---
title: "Test Merge House Price Data"
author: "Jacopo Olivieri"
date: "2024-01-31"
output:
  html_document:
    fig_width: 6
    fig_aspect_ratio: 0.618
knitr:
  opts_chunk:
    echo: false
    warning: false
    message: false
    fig.align: "center"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Initialize packages with version control
if (!requireNamespace("renv", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("renv")
}

# Run manual renv commands if needed
# renv::activate() # Activate the project library # nolint
# renv::restore()  # Restore the environment if running for first time # nolint
# renv::snapshot()  # After adding/updating packages, snapshot the state # nolint


# Load required packages
required_packages <- c(
  "rmarkdown", "rio", "tidyverse", "purrr", "here",
  "janitor", "data.table", "lubridate", "rnrfa", "sf",
  "furrr", "future", "tmap"
)
install_if_missing <- function(packages) {
  new_packages <- packages[!sapply(packages, requireNamespace, quietly = TRUE)]
  if (length(new_packages) > 0) {
    install.packages(new_packages)
  }
  invisible(sapply(packages, library, character.only = TRUE))
}
install_if_missing(required_packages)

# set up parallel processing
setup_parallel <- function() {
  n_workers <- parallelly::availableCores() - 1
  machine_ram <- memuse::Sys.meminfo()$totalram
  ram_limit <- as.numeric(0.7 * machine_ram)

  options(future.globals.maxSize = ram_limit)
  future::plan(future::multisession, workers = n_workers)
  logger::log_info("Parallel processing initialized with {n_workers} workers")
}
setup_parallel()


# Initialize logging
log_path <- here::here("output", "log", "test_merge_house_price_data.log")
if (!dir.exists(dirname(log_path))) {
  dir.create(dirname(log_path), recursive = TRUE)
}
logger::log_appender(logger::appender_file(log_path))
logger::log_layout(logger::layout_glue_colors)
```

## Load Data

### Load EDM Data

```{r, load_data}
dat <- import(here(
  "data", "processed",
  "individual_edm_location.RDATA"
), trust = TRUE)

dat_yr <- dat$yearly
dat_mo <- dat$monthly
```

### Load House Price Data

```{r}
house_price_data <- import(here("data", "processed", "house_price.rds"),
  trust = TRUE
)
spill_sites <- import(here("data", "processed", "unique_spill_sites.rds"),
  trust = TRUE
)
# spill_lookup <- import(here("data", "processed", "spill_lookup.rds"),
#   trust = TRUE
# )
```

## Merge Data

### Prepare unique spill sites

```{r}
set.seed(1)
spill_sites_sample <- spill_sites %>%
  slice_sample(n = 4000) %>%
  st_as_sf(coords = c("easting", "northing"), crs = 27700)

spill_sites_sf <- spill_sites_sample %>%
  rename(spill_geom = geometry)
```

### Prepare House Price Data

```{r}
houses_sf <- house_price_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(easting) & !is.na(northing)) %>%
  mutate(house_id = row_number()) %>%
  st_as_sf(coords = c("easting", "northing"), crs = 27700) %>%
  select(house_id, transaction_id, year, geometry)
```

### Merge Data by Chunk

```{r}
# Set matching radius (in kilometres)
radius_km <- 10
radius_m <- radius_km * 1000

# Prepare spill site data for the join:
spill_sites_sf <- spill_sites_sample %>%
  rename(spill_geom = geometry)

# Map each house sale to every spill site within the defined radius.
house_spill_matches <- st_join(houses_sf, spill_sites_sf,
  join = st_is_within_distance,
  dist = radius_m,
  left = FALSE
)

# Reattach the spill site's geometry using a lookup table (by site_id)
spill_lookup <- spill_sites_sf %>%
  st_set_geometry(NULL) %>% # Remove active geometry to avoid join issues
  mutate(spill_geom = spill_sites_sf$spill_geom) %>%
  select(site_id, spill_geom)

house_spill_matches <- house_spill_matches %>%
  left_join(spill_lookup, by = "site_id")

# Calculate the straight-line (Euclidean) distance for each house–spill pair
house_spill_matches <- house_spill_matches %>%
  mutate(
    distance_m = as.numeric(st_distance(geometry, spill_geom, by_element = TRUE)),
    distance_km = distance_m / 1000
  )
```


### Sample

Sanity check
```{r}
small_spill_sites <- spill_sites_sample %>%
  slice_sample(n = 50)

matched_houses <- house_spill_matches_sample %>%
  filter(site_id %in% small_spill_sites$site_id) %>%
  distinct(house_id, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  left_join(house_price_data)

# Create 10 km buffers around the house and spill site geometries
houses_buffer <- st_buffer(matched_houses, dist = 10000)
small_spill_buffer <- st_buffer(small_spill_sites, dist = 10000)

tmap_mode("view")
map <- tm_shape(small_spill_buffer) +
  tm_polygons(fill = "lightgreen", fill_alpha = 0.3, col_alpha = 0) +
  tm_shape(matched_houses) +
  tm_dots(fill = "darkblue", size = .5, fill_alpha = 0.8) +
  tm_shape(small_spill_sites) +
  tm_dots(fill = "red", size = 1, fill_alpha = 0.9) +
  tm_title("Matched House Sales & Spill Sites with 10km Buffers") +
  tm_layout(legend.outside = TRUE)
map

tm_shape(small_spill_buffer) +
  tm_polygons(
    fill = "lightgreen",
    fill_alpha = 0.1,
    col_alpha = 0
  ) +
  tm_shape(matched_houses) +
  tm_bubbles(
    size = "price",
    fill = "price",
    fill.scale = tm_scale_intervals(
      values = "brewer.blues",
      n = 8
    ),
    size.scale = tm_scale_continuous(
      values.scale = 3
    ),
    size.legend = tm_legend_hide(),
    fill.legend = tm_legend(
      title = "Price Paid (?)"
    )
  ) +
  tm_shape(small_spill_sites) +
  tm_dots(
    fill = "red",
    size = 1,
    fill_alpha = 0.8
  ) +
  tm_title("Matched House Sales & Spill Sites with 10km Buffers") +
  tm_layout(legend.outside = TRUE)
```


### All observations

```{r}
chunk_size <- 1000

# Split house_sample into a list of chunks
house_chunks <- split(
  houses_sf,
  (seq_len(nrow(houses_sf)) - 1) %/% chunk_size
)

# Function to process each chunk:
#   - Perform the spatial join on the chunk with spill_sites_sf
#   - Reattach spill_geom from the lookup table
#   - Calculate the straight-line distance for each match
process_chunk <- function(chunk) {
  joined <- st_join(chunk, spill_sites_sf,
    join = st_is_within_distance,
    dist = radius_m,
    left = FALSE
  )

  joined <- joined %>%
    left_join(spill_lookup, by = "site_id") %>%
    mutate(
      distance_m = as.numeric(
        st_distance(geometry, spill_geom, by_element = TRUE)
      ),
      distance_km = distance_m / 1000
    )

  return(joined)
}

# Process each chunk in parallel and combine the results into one long-format table.
house_spill_matches <- future_map_dfr(house_chunks, process_chunk,
  .progress = TRUE,
  .options = furrr_options(seed = TRUE)
)
saveRDS(house_spill_matches, here("data", "processed", "spill_lookup.rds"))
```





### Archive

```{r}
process_chunk <- function(chunk) {
  chunk %>%
    st_join(
      spill_sites,
      join = st_is_within_distance,
      dist = 10000 # 10km buffer
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      distance = as.numeric(st_distance(geometry.x, geometry.y, by_element = TRUE))
    ) %>%
    st_drop_geometry() %>%
    select(house_id, site_id, distance, water_company)
}

results <- seq(1, nrow(house_sample), by = 1000) %>%
  future_map_dfr(function(i) {
    chunk <- house_sample[i:pmin(i + 999, nrow(house_sample)), ]
    gc()
    process_chunk(chunk)
  }, .progress = TRUE, .options = furrr_options(seed = TRUE))
```

```{r}
test_chunk <- house_sample[1:1000, ] # First 1000 houses

# Process the chunk
test_results <- test_chunk %>%
  st_join(
    spill_sites,
    join = st_is_within_distance,
    dist = 10000 # 10km buffer
  ) %>%
  # After st_join, we just use 'geometry' as the column name
  mutate(
    distance = as.numeric(st_distance(geometry, spill_sites[match(site_id, spill_sites$site_id), ], by_element = TRUE))
  )
```

### Parallelisation testing

```{r}
# --- Chunk Size Performance Test ---

house_price_data <- import(here("data", "processed", "house_price.rds"),
  trust = TRUE
)
spill_sites <- import(here("data", "processed", "unique_spill_sites.rds"),
  trust = TRUE
)

spill_sites_sf <- spill_sites %>%
  # slice_sample(n = 4000) %>%
  st_as_sf(coords = c("easting", "northing"), crs = 27700) %>% 
  select(site_id, geometry) %>%
  rename(spill_geom = geometry)

houses_sf <- house_price_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(easting) & !is.na(northing)) %>%
  mutate(house_id = row_number()) %>%
  st_as_sf(coords = c("easting", "northing"), crs = 27700) %>%
  select(house_id, geometry)

# Set matching radius (in kilometres)
radius_km <- 10
radius_m <- radius_km * 1000

# Process chunk function (mimicking the one used in your test script):
# Reduce spill_sites_sf for testing by taking a random sample of 100 sites.
# Load required libraries:
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(furrr)
library(ggplot2)
library(microbenchmark)

# The following objects must be defined beforehand:
#   • house_sample: an sf object with house sale data (with geometry)
#   • spill_sites_sf: an sf object with sewage spill site data (with geometry)
#   • spill_lookup: a lookup tibble providing additional spill site details 
#                   (includes a "site_id" column and matching geometry column "spill_geom")
#   • radius_m: matching radius in metres (e.g. 10000 for 10 km)

# Reduce spill_sites_sf for testing by taking a random sample of 300 sites.
set.seed(123)  # for reproducibility
spill_sites_sf_sample <- spill_sites_sf #%>% slice_sample(n = 300)

# Define the function to process a chunk of houses.
process_chunk <- function(chunk) {
  # Perform a spatial join to find houses within the specified radius of any spill site.
  joined <- st_join(chunk, spill_sites_sf,
                    join = st_is_within_distance,
                    dist = radius_m,
                    left = FALSE)
  # Optionally, you can join additional lookup details here if needed.
  return(joined)
}

# Define candidate chunk sizes based on our discussion:
candidate_chunk_sizes <- c(100, 500, 1000)

# Data frame to store performance results for both methods.
performance_results <- tibble(
  method = character(),
  chunk_size = integer(),
  num_chunks = integer(),
  execution_time_sec = numeric()
)

# Loop over each candidate chunk size.
for (cs in candidate_chunk_sizes) {
  # Split the houses_sf data into chunks based on the current chunk size.
  house_chunks <- split(
    houses_sf,
    (seq_len(nrow(houses_sf)) - 1) %/% cs
  )
  
  num_chunks <- length(house_chunks)
  cat("Testing with chunk size:", cs, "->", num_chunks, "chunks.\n")
  
  # Measure the sequential processing time using purrr::map_dfr.
  mb_seq <- microbenchmark(
    seq_result <- map_dfr(house_chunks, process_chunk),
    times = 1
  )
  elapsed_seq <- median(mb_seq$time) / 1e9
  cat("Sequential (map_dfr) for chunk size", cs, "took", elapsed_seq, "seconds.\n")
  
  # performance_results <- performance_results %>%
  #   add_row(method = "sequential", chunk_size = cs, num_chunks = num_chunks, execution_time_sec = elapsed_seq)
  
  # Measure the parallel processing time using furrr::future_map_dfr.
  mb_par <- microbenchmark(
    par_result <- future_map_dfr(house_chunks, process_chunk,
                                 .progress = FALSE,
                                 .options = furrr::furrr_options(seed = TRUE)),
    times = 1
  )
  elapsed_par <- median(mb_par$time) / 1e9
  cat("Parallel (future_map_dfr) for chunk size", cs, "took", elapsed_par, "seconds.\n\n")
  
  performance_results <- performance_results %>%
    add_row(method = "parallel", chunk_size = cs, num_chunks = num_chunks, execution_time_sec = elapsed_par)
}

# Display the performance results.
print(performance_results)

# Plot the benchmark results.
ggplot(performance_results, aes(x = as.factor(chunk_size), y = execution_time_sec, fill = method)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  labs(
    title = "Benchmarking: Chunk Size vs Execution Time",
    x = "Chunk Size",
    y = "Execution Time (seconds)",
    fill = "Method"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
# ggsave(here("output", "code_testing", "spill_sites_house_sales_merge_parallelisation.png"))

```

```{r}
############################################################
# Merge House Price Data with Nearby Spill Sites
# Project: Spatial Data Integration
# Date: 04/02/2025
# Author: Jacopo Olivieri
############################################################

# Set Up
############################################################

#' Initialise the R environment with required packages and settings
#' @return NULL
initialise_environment <- function() {
  # Package management with renv
  if (!requireNamespace("renv", quietly = TRUE)) {
    install.packages("renv")
    renv::init()
  }

  # Define required packages
  required_packages <- c(
    "rmarkdown", "rio", "tidyverse", "purrr", "here", "logger", "glue", "fs", "rnrfa", "sf"
  )

  # Install and load packages
  invisible(sapply(required_packages, function(pkg) {
    if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {
      install.packages(pkg)
    }
    library(pkg, character.only = TRUE)
  }))
}

#' Set up logging configuration
#' @return NULL
setup_logging <- function() {
  log_path <- here::here("output", "log", "10_10km_site_house_sale_match.log")
  dir.create(dirname(log_path), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

  logger::log_appender(logger::appender_file(log_path))
  logger::log_layout(logger::layout_glue_colors)
  logger::log_threshold(logger::DEBUG)
  logger::log_info("Script started at {Sys.time()}")
}

############################################################
# Configuration
############################################################

CONFIG <- list(
  processed_dir = here::here("data", "processed")
)

############################################################
# Functions
############################################################

#' Load spatial datasets for house prices and spill sites.
#' @return List containing house price data and spill sites data
load_data <- function() {
  logger::log_info("Loading spatial datasets")

  house_data <- rio::import(here::here("data", "processed", "house_price.rds"),
    trust = TRUE
  )
  spill_data <- rio::import(here::here("data", "processed", "unique_spill_sites.rds"),
    trust = TRUE
  )

  return(list(house = house_data, spill = spill_data))
}

#' Prepare spill sites spatial data and create a lookup table.
#' @param spill_data DataFrame of spill sites
#' @return List containing the spatial spill sites and a lookup table
prepare_spill_sites <- function(spill_data) {
  logger::log_info("Preparing spill sites spatial data")

  spill_sites_sf <- spill_data %>%
    st_as_sf(coords = c("easting", "northing"), crs = 27700) %>%
    rename(spill_geom = geometry)

  spill_lookup <- spill_sites_sf %>%
    st_set_geometry(NULL) %>%
    mutate(spill_geom = spill_sites_sf$spill_geom) %>%
    select(site_id, spill_geom)

  return(list(spill_sf = spill_sites_sf, lookup = spill_lookup))
}

#' Prepare house price spatial data.
#' @param house_data DataFrame of house price records
#' @return sf object containing the house price data
prepare_house_data <- function(house_data) {
  logger::log_info("Preparing house price spatial data")

  houses_sf <- house_data %>%
    filter(!is.na(easting) & !is.na(northing)) %>%
    mutate(house_id = row_number()) %>%
    st_as_sf(coords = c("easting", "northing"), crs = 27700) %>%
    select(house_id, geometry)

  return(houses_sf)
}

#' Perform spatial join to match houses with nearby spill sites.
#' @param houses_sf sf object containing house price data
#' @param spill_sites_sf sf object containing spill sites
#' @param spill_lookup Lookup table for spill sites
#' @param radius_km Numeric radius in kilometres for matching (default: 10)
#' @return sf object with merged house and spill data, including distance calculations
perform_spatial_join <- function(houses_sf, spill_sites_sf, spill_lookup, radius_km = 10) {
  logger::log_info("Performing spatial join between houses and spill sites")
  
  radius_m <- radius_km * 1000
  chunk_size <- 2000
  
  house_chunks <- split(
    houses_sf,
    (seq_len(nrow(houses_sf)) - 1) %/% chunk_size
  )
  
  merged_chunks <- purrr::map(house_chunks, function(chunk) {
    logger::log_info(glue::glue("Processing chunk with {nrow(chunk)} houses"))
    
    st_join(chunk, spill_sites_sf,
            join = st_is_within_distance,
            dist = radius_m,
            left = TRUE
    ) %>%
      left_join(spill_lookup, by = "site_id") %>%
      mutate(
        # Calculate the straight-line distance to the spill site
        distance_m = if_else(is.na(spill_geom), NA_real_,
                             as.numeric(st_distance(geometry, spill_geom, by_element = TRUE))
        ),
        distance_km = distance_m / 1000
      ) %>%
      group_by(house_id) %>%
      mutate(n_discharge_outlet = sum(!is.na(site_id))) %>%
      ungroup() %>%
      st_drop_geometry() %>%
      select(house_id, site_id, transaction_id, year, distance_m, distance_km, n_discharge_outlet)
  })
  
  merged_data <- dplyr::bind_rows(merged_chunks)
  return(merged_data)
}


#' Process the spatial data by loading, preparing, and merging house price and spill site data.
#' @param radius_km Numeric radius in kilometres for the spatial join (default: 10)
#' @return sf object containing the merged data
process_spatial_data <- function(radius_km = 10) {
  datasets <- load_data()

  houses_sf <- prepare_house_data(datasets$house)
  spill_data <- prepare_spill_sites(datasets$spill)

  merged_spatial_data <- perform_spatial_join(houses_sf, spill_data$spill_sf, spill_data$lookup, radius_km)

  return(merged_spatial_data)
}

# New export function added here
export_data <- function(df) {
  # Define output paths
  rds_path <- file.path(CONFIG$processed_dir, "spill_house_lookup.rds")
  csv_path <- file.path(CONFIG$processed_dir, "spill_house_lookup.csv")

  # Export data with error handling
  tryCatch(
    {
      saveRDS(df, rds_path)
      write_csv(df, csv_path)

      logger::log_info(glue::glue("Data exported successfully to:"))
      logger::log_info(glue::glue("  - RDS: {rds_path}"))
      logger::log_info(glue::glue("  - CSV: {csv_path}"))
    },
    error = function(e) {
      err_msg <- glue::glue("Failed to export data: {e$message}")
      logger::log_error(err_msg)
      stop(err_msg)
    }
  )
}

############################################################
# Main Execution
############################################################

#' Main function to execute the spatial data integration pipeline.
#' @return Invisible NULL
main <- function() {
  tryCatch(
    {
      initialise_environment()
      setup_logging()

      logger::log_info("Starting spatial data processing pipeline")
      merged_data <- process_spatial_data()
      logger::log_info("Spatial join completed successfully")

      # Export the merged data using the export_data function
      logger::log_info("Exporting data")
      export_data(merged_data)
    },
    error = function(e) {
      logger::log_error(glue::glue("Pipeline failed: {e$message}"))
      stop(e)
    }
  )

  invisible(NULL)
}

if (sys.nframe() == 0) {
  main()
}

```
